exclusion_rules.description: Only patients with End Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) or chronic kidney disease requiring maintenance dialysis are eligible
Must have a prescription for maintenance hemodialysis from a qualified nephrologist or physician

family_criteria.family_definition: Individual entitlement for any patient diagnosed with ESRD requiring maintenance hemodialysis. No income, caste, or family restriction. BPL patients get completely free dialysis; APL patients may pay subsidized rates in some states.

covered_procedures: Maintenance hemodialysis sessions (typically 2-3 sessions per week)
Dialysis consumables (dialyzer, blood tubing, fistula needles, dialysis fluid)
Essential drugs during dialysis (erythropoietin, iron sucrose, heparin, etc.)
Blood pressure and vital monitoring during sessions
Basic blood investigations (Hb, creatinine, electrolytes, hepatitis screening)
Free dialysis for BPL patients at PPP centres
Subsidized dialysis for APL patients at PPP centres
excluded_procedures: Peritoneal dialysis (CAPD) — covered under separate programme in some states
Dialysis at private centres not under the PPP model
Treatment of CKD not requiring dialysis

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: For patient registration and tracking at the dialysis centre

required_documents.name: Nephrologist/Physician Prescription
required_documents.notes: Prescription confirming ESRD diagnosis and need for maintenance hemodialysis

required_documents.name: BPL Card / Income Certificate
required_documents.notes: For availing completely free dialysis. APL patients may need to pay subsidized charges in some states.

required_documents.name: Blood Test Reports
required_documents.notes: Recent creatinine, BUN, electrolytes, hepatitis B/C screening, HIV test reports

enrollment_channels: Government medical college hospital nephrology department
Referral from Primary Health Centre or CHC
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Get diagnosed with ESRD/CKD by a nephrologist or physician at a government hospital
Step 2: Obtain prescription for maintenance hemodialysis with frequency recommendation
Step 3: Visit nearest PMNDP dialysis centre (at district hospital or empanelled PPP centre)
Step 4: Register with Aadhaar and medical documents at the dialysis centre
Step 5: BPL patients registered for free dialysis; APL patients for subsidized sessions
Step 6: Undergo initial assessment including blood tests and hepatitis/HIV screening
Step 7: Start regular dialysis sessions (typically 2-3 per week, 4 hours each)
Step 8: Regular follow-up blood tests and clinical monitoring at the centre
description_for_embedding: Pradhan Mantri National Dialysis Programme (PMNDP) is a central government programme providing free or subsidized hemodialysis to patients with End Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) at 1700+ dialysis centres across 751 districts in India, operated primarily through a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model at district hospitals. BPL patients receive completely free dialysis including consumables and essential drugs. No income criteria for access — universal for all ESRD patients needing maintenance dialysis. Requires nephrologist prescription. Does not cover kidney transplant (covered under PM-JAY/RAN) or peritoneal dialysis.
